key_insight: The paper shows that deterministic single-loop second-order bilevel optimization can achieve the optimal \u039f(\u03b5^{-1.5}) complexity for finding an \u03b5-second-order stationary point, even when the induced hyperfunction is nonconvex.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical, theoretically optimal path for bilevel models in machine learning and engineering design where fast, reliable hyperparameter or design optimization is needed.
method: Build a double-loop CRN baseline to establish the optimal outer convergence rate.
Propose a single-loop cubic regularized Newton method with one lower-level gradient step plus one Newton step for the hypergradient.
Analyze deterministic oracle complexity and prove \u039f(\u03b5^{-1.5}) total cost for the NCSC bilevel setting.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical convergence and oracle-complexity analysis, culminating in an optimal deterministic \u039f(\u03b5^{-1.5}) rate for an \u03b5-SOSP, rather than an empirical benchmark study.
watch_item: The method depends on accurate hypergradient and hyper-Hessian approximations, and seemingly minor algorithmic modifications may break the convergence guarantee.
paper_title: On Second-Order Methods for Bilevel Optimization
